2603.17275.title: DANCE: Dynamic 3D CNN Pruning: Joint Frame, Channel, and Feature Adaptation for Energy Efficiency on the Edge
2603.17275.paper_summary: Modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are workhorses for video and image processing, but fail to adapt to the computational complexity of input samples in a dynamic manner to minimize energy consumption. In this research, we propose DANCE, a fine-grained, input-aware, dynamic pruning framework for 3D CNNs to maximize power efficiency with negligible to zero impact on performance. In the proposed two-step approach, the first step is called activation variability amplification (AVA), and the 3D CNN model is retrained to increase the variance of the magnitude of neuron activations across the network in this step, facilitating pruning decisions across diverse CNN input scenarios. In the second step, called adaptive activation pruning (AAP), a lightweight activation controller network is trained to dynamically prune frames, channels, and features of 3D convolutional layers of the network (different for each layer), based on statistics of the outputs of the first layer of the network. Our method achieves substantial savings in multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations and memory accesses by introducing sparsity within convolutional layers. Hardware validation on the NVIDIA Jetson Nano GPU and the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 platform demonstrates respective speedups of 1.37X and 2.22X, achieving up to 1.47X higher energy efficiency compared to the state of the art.
2603.17275.paper_summary_zh: 現代卷積神經網絡（CNN）是視頻和圖像處理的主力軍，但未能以動態方式適應輸入樣本的計算複雜性，以最小化能耗。在本研究中，我們提出了DANCE，一個細粒度的、基於輸入的動態修剪框架，用於3D CNN，以最大化電力效率，對性能的影響可忽略不計。在所提出的兩步驟方法中，第一步稱為激活變異性增強（AVA），在這一步中，3D CNN模型被重新訓練，以增加神經元激活幅度在整個網絡中的變異性，從而促進在不同CNN輸入場景中的修剪決策。在第二步，稱為自適應激活修剪（AAP），訓練了一個輕量級的激活控制器網絡，以根據網絡第一層輸出的統計數據，動態修剪網絡的3D卷積層的幀、通道和特徵（每層不同）。我們的方法通過在卷積層內引入稀疏性，實現了在乘加（MAC）操作和內存訪問方面的顯著節省。在NVIDIA Jetson Nano GPU和高通Snapdragon 8 Gen 1平台上的硬件驗證顯示，分別實現了1.37倍和2.22倍的加速，相較於當前最先進技術，達到了高達1.47倍的能效提升。
2603.17275.author: Mohamed Mejri et.al.
2603.17275.authors: Mohamed Mejri, Ashiqur Rasul, Abhijit Chatterjee
